title: Jamaican Beef Patties

overview.description: Flaky golden pastry filled with richly seasoned Jamaican beef, onion and aromatic spices.

ingredients: 1 Scotch bonnet chilli, very finely chopped
method: Mix the flour, salt, curry powder and turmeric together in a large bowl.
Rub in the cold butter and beef dripping or lard until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs.
Add the ice-cold water gradually, mixing until a firm dough forms.
Bring the dough together without kneading.
Flatten into a disc, wrap and chill for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened.
Add the garlic, Scotch bonnet, spring onions, curry powder, thyme and allspice.
Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the beef mince and cook until browned, breaking it up thoroughly.
Add the beef stock and simmer until almost completely reduced.
Stir in the breadcrumbs to absorb any remaining moisture.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Allow the filling to cool completely.
Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan).
Divide the pastry into 8 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a circle approximately 15cm in diameter.
Place a portion of the cooled beef filling on one half of each pastry circle.
Fold the pastry over the filling to form a half-moon.
Press the edges firmly together, then crimp with a fork.
Place the patties on a baking tray.
Brush with beaten egg.
Bake for 25–30 minutes until golden and crisp.
Allow to cool for a few minutes before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Jamaican beef patties developed from the influence of Cornish pasties, Indian spices and Jamaican ingredients, creating a pastry snack that is now one of Jamaica's most recognisable foods.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a richly seasoned minced beef filling, work with pastry and shape filled pastries for batch cooking.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A substantial freezer-friendly snack that combines a flaky, warmly spiced pastry with a deeply seasoned beef filling.
chapman-tips: The filling must be cool before it goes into the pastry or the pastry will become difficult to work with.
Cook the filling until it is fairly dry. Excess moisture will make the pastry soggy.
Keep the pastry cold while working with it.
The Scotch bonnet adds both heat and fruity flavour. Adjust the amount to suit your family.
Make the patties the same size so they cook evenly.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the assembled, unbaked patties on a tray until solid, then transfer to a freezer bag or container. Bake from frozen at 200°C (180°C fan), adding approximately 10–15 minutes to the cooking time.
notes.leftovers: Cooked patties can be refrigerated and reheated in the oven. They are also good cold in packed lunches.
